celebration: or and ore both say /or/ — the second r-controlled sound (corn, fork, more, store). And a word the class has wanted since Level 1 finally arrives: FOR is decodable at last (f + or). Right-branch leaf #6.

songs.title: Or-Or-Corn!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(or… or… or…)
Or! Or! Corn!

[Hook]
The R is BOSSY! (orrr!)
O plus R says /or/! (orrr!)
Or-or-corn! Or-or-corn!
Two letters, one sound! (or!)

[Verse 2]
C-O-R-N — corn! (eat the corn!)
F-O-R-K — fork! (on the fork!)
Or-or-corn! Or-or-corn!
The bossy R says /or/! (or!)

[Verse 3]
More! More! (more corn!)
M-O-R-E — more! (at the store!)
Or-or-corn! Or-or-corn!
More corn for the horse!

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not corn!
(or… or… or…)

[Outro]
Corn… fork… more… (shhh…)
Or! Or! Corn!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Second r-controlled song — growl the /or/ (phonetic respelling: sung 'or', printed or/ore). The lesson: or in the middle (corn, fork), ore at the end (more, store). Spell-outs uppercase. The bridge quietly celebrates that 'for' is finally readable. Potato is not corn — the class knows the difference by now.

songs.title: More Corn for the Horse
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
More corn for the horse!
More corn for the horse!
More! More! (more corn!)
More corn for the horse!

[Verse 2]
The horse can eat the corn!
The corn is on the fork!
More! More! (more corn!)
More corn for the horse!

[Verse 3]
The corn is in the store!
Star can see the corn!
More! More! (more corn!)
More corn for the horse!

[Outro]
The corn… the horse… (shhh…)
For the horse?! (a potato!)
(POTATO!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'More corn for the horse.' — 'horse' and 'for' are both new-this-week or freshly unlocked. A warm barnyard bop. 'eat' carries the ea team, 'see' the ee team. Whisper the outro; potato hits loud in the feed bucket.

spreads.artNote: A golden ear of corn alone in the spotlight, husk half-peeled, kernels gleaming.
A gentle brown horse looking hopefully at the corn, ears forward. A story friend — 'horse' is fully decodable now.
The horse happily munching an ear of corn, kernels flying, eyes half-closed in bliss.
The horse looking up eagerly, wanting more, a second ear of corn just out of reach on a shelf.
A big pitchfork leaning in the spotlight — the tool for lifting more corn.

spreads.text: The corn is on the fork.
spreads.artNote: An ear of corn balanced on the tines of the pitchfork, lifted up toward the hopeful horse.
The horse being served a whole armful of corn, delighted, tail swishing. 'for' finally in print.

spreads.text: The corn is in the store.
spreads.artNote: A little farm store with sacks and shelves — and more corn than any horse could dream of. Star glows in the window.
In the horse's feed bucket where the corn should be sits a single potato, looking pleased with itself. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. The horse gives it a long, doubtful stare.

sentences: The corn is on the fork.
The corn is in the store.
extras: THE SECOND R-CONTROLLED VOWEL: o plus r says /or/. or lives in the MIDDLE (corn, fork, horn, storm); ore lives at the END (more, store, core, snore). Both are the bossy R again, this time bending o. Sort by position.
THE 'for' CELEBRATION: 'for' is a word the children have wanted to write since Level 1 — today it is finally decodable (f + or). Make a small fuss; write it on the wall.
TEACH ONE BOSSY-R AT A TIME: ar was last week, or is this week — do NOT introduce er/ir/ur (that is next week, and it is the big two-syllable gate).
Right-branch leaf #6 goes up: read corn/fork/more/store aloud, hang the or/ore leaf.
Moveable alphabet: build c-or-n, f-or-k, m-or-e, s-t-or-e — weld or as one piece; add the silent e for ore.

teacherFocus: THE SECOND R-CONTROLLED VOWEL. o plus r says /or/: or in the middle (corn, fork, horn, storm), ore at the end (more, store, core, snore). Same bossy R, new vowel. Celebrate 'for' — decodable at last (f + or), a word wanted since Level 1. Keep to one bossy-r sound: ar last week, or this week; er/ir/ur wait for next week's big two-syllable gate. Hang right-branch leaf #6.
